$schema: Manifest ↔ Prisma schema-drift allowlist. See scripts/manifest/audit-schema-drift.mjs for semantics.

_doc.globalAdapterDerived: Cross-cutting fields that EVERY Prisma store adapter injects from runtime context, not from command input. Applies to every governed entity.
_doc.adapterDerived: Per-entity fields that the entity's Prisma store adapter derives via a documented rule (e.g. looking up a parent entity, normalizing case from manifest camelCase to Prisma snake_case).
_doc.nonconforming: Per constitution §14, acknowledged tech debt with an active removal plan. Bypass is not permission.
_doc.ignoredEntities: Entity names to skip entirely (e.g. infrastructure tables incorrectly tagged as governed).
_doc.ignoredFields: Per-entity field allowlist for fields the audit should not enforce.

tenantId.rule: Every Prisma store adapter injects tenantId from RuntimeContext.user.tenantId. See packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-store.ts where each .create() sets `tenantId: this.tenantId`. Constitution §19 requires runtime tenant context on every governed command.

first_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `first_name` (no @map); manifest declares camelCase `firstName`. ClientContactPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch04-client-trio.ts normalizes via `first_name: asString(data.first_name ?? data.firstName)`. Manifest-driven flow always passes `firstName`. Tech-debt note: the cleaner long-term fix is renaming the Prisma field to `firstName @map("first_name")` so the schema convention is uniform; deferred to avoid a Prisma-first change in violation of constitution §14 ordering.

last_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `last_name` (no @map); manifest declares camelCase `lastName`. Same adapter handling as `first_name` in ClientContactPrismaStore (broken-read-batch04-client-trio.ts).

board_name.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `board_name` (no @map); manifest declares `boardName`. BattleBoardPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch02) normalizes via `board_name: asString(data.board_name ?? data.boardName)`.

customer_id.rule: Prisma field is `customer_id @db.Uuid` (no @map); manifest declares `customerId`. CateringOrderPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch03) normalizes via `customer_id: asString(data.customer_id ?? data.customerId)`.

delivery_date.rule: Prisma field is `delivery_date DateTime` (no @map); manifest declares `deliveryDate: number`. Adapter normalizes via `data.delivery_date ?? data.deliveryDate` and converts to Date.

delivery_time.rule: Prisma field is `delivery_time String` (no @map); manifest declares `deliveryTime`. Adapter normalizes via `data.delivery_time ?? data.deliveryTime`.

supplier_number.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `supplier_number` (no @map); manifest declares `supplierNumber`. InventorySupplierPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch09) normalizes via `supplier_number: asString(data.supplierNumber ?? data.supplier_number)`.

trigger_type.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `trigger_type` (no @map); manifest declares `triggerType`. WorkflowPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch01) normalizes via `trigger_type: asString(data.trigger_type ?? data.triggerType)`.

recipient_employee_id.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `recipient_employee_id @db.Uuid` (no @map); manifest declares `recipientEmployeeId`. NotificationPrismaStore.create reads `data.recipientEmployeeId` and writes to `recipient_employee_id`. One-direction (manifest→Prisma) is correct for the dispatcher path; raw snake_case input is not supported and not needed because all governed flows go through the manifest dispatcher.

notification_type.rule: Prisma field is literal snake_case `notification_type` (no @map); manifest declares `notificationType`. Same one-direction adapter pattern as `recipient_employee_id`.

schedule_date.rule: Prisma field is `schedule_date DateTime @db.Date` (no @map); manifest declares `scheduleDate: number`. SchedulePrismaStore.create reads `data.scheduleDate` and writes to `schedule_date` (one-direction; manifest dispatcher only).

shift_start.rule: Prisma field is `shift_start DateTime @db.Timestamptz(6)` (no @map); manifest declares `shiftStart: number`. ScheduleShiftPrismaStore.create (broken-read-batch13) normalizes via `data.shift_start ?? data.shiftStart` and converts to Date.

shift_end.rule: Prisma field is `shift_end DateTime @db.Timestamptz(6)` (no @map); manifest declares `shiftEnd: number`. Same adapter handling as `shift_start` in ScheduleShiftPrismaStore.

startTime.rule: Prisma field is `startTime DateTime @map("start_time") @db.Time(6)` — time-of-day storage, NOT a full DateTime/instant. Manifest declares `startTime: string = ""` because @db.Time(6) values are pass-through strings (e.g. "09:00:00"); coercing to epoch ms would lose semantics (date component is irrelevant for time-of-day). EmployeeAvailabilityPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch06-employee-availability.ts:53 passes `data.startTime as string` directly. One-direction (manifest dispatcher → Prisma); the type_mismatch flag is a false positive of the audit's blanket DateTime→number convention. Long-term cleanup: either teach the audit script that @db.Time(6) should expect manifest `string`, or split EmployeeAvailability.startTime/endTime into HH:MM:SS string properties (already what they are). reviewBy is unchanged from the existing allowlist cadence.

endTime.rule: Prisma field is `endTime DateTime @map("end_time") @db.Time(6)` — time-of-day storage, NOT a full DateTime/instant. Same handling as `startTime` in EmployeeAvailabilityPrismaStore (broken-read-batch06-employee-availability.ts:54). Pass-through string; no Date coercion appropriate.

issuedDate.rule: Prisma field is `issuedDate DateTime @map("issued_date") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Manifest declares `issuedDate: string = ""` (ISO date format "YYYY-MM-DD" expected); Prisma 7 accepts ISO date strings for @db.Date fields without coercion. EmployeeCertification has no dedicated PrismaStore — falls back to the generic adapter, which passes the string through. One-direction (manifest dispatcher → Prisma). Long-term tech debt: align manifest type to `number` (epoch ms) per the convention established by PayrollPeriod/TimeOffRequest, requires adding an EmployeeCertificationPrismaStore that does `new Date(epochMs)` coercion.

periodStart.rule: Prisma field is `periodStart DateTime @map("period_start") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Manifest declares `periodStart: string = ""` (ISO date format). PayrollPeriodPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch11-payroll.ts:170-174 coerces via `data.periodStart ? new Date(data.periodStart) : new Date()` (Date constructor accepts both ISO string and epoch ms). mapToManifestEntity:237-239 converts Date→epoch ms on read, so the read shape is `number` even though the write shape is `string`. Long-term tech debt: align manifest write type to `number` so write/read shapes match the convention established by CateringOrder.delivery_date, Schedule.schedule_date, ScheduleShift.shift_start/shift_end. Requires API route transformBody + UI alignment; out of scope for this allowlist pass.

periodEnd.rule: Prisma field is `periodEnd DateTime @map("period_end") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Same handling as `periodStart` in PayrollPeriodPrismaStore (broken-read-batch11-payroll.ts:175-178). Write accepts string; read returns epoch ms.

effectiveDate.rule: Prisma field is `effectiveDate DateTime @map("effective_date") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Manifest declares `effectiveDate: string = ""` (ISO date format). EmployeeDeduction has no dedicated PrismaStore yet; generic adapter passes the string through and Prisma 7 accepts ISO date strings for @db.Date fields. One-direction (manifest dispatcher → Prisma). Long-term tech debt: same as EmployeeCertification.issuedDate — align manifest to `number` once a dedicated adapter exists.

startDate.rule: Prisma field is `startDate DateTime @map("start_date") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Manifest declares `startDate: string = ""` (ISO date format). TimeOffRequestPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch15-rolepolicy-timeoff.ts:173 coerces via `asNullableDate(data.startDate) ?? new Date()` (asNullableDate accepts ISO strings, epoch ms, and Date instances; see asNullableDate helper). Long-term tech debt: align manifest write type to `number` per the established convention; requires the API route transformBody at apps/api/app/api/staff/time-off/requests/route.ts to convert ISO→epoch ms (currently passes string through from request body) plus UI alignment.

endDate.rule: Prisma field is `endDate DateTime @map("end_date") @db.Date` — date-only storage. Same handling as `startDate` in TimeOffRequestPrismaStore (broken-read-batch15-rolepolicy-timeoff.ts:174).

preferenceValue.rule: Prisma field is `preferenceValue Json @map("preference_value")` — JSON storage. Manifest declares `preferenceValue: string = ""` because the manifest IR grammar (packages/manifest-ir/dsl/grammar.peg) has no `object` kind yet — `string|number|boolean` are the only scalar property types available. ClientPreferencePrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch04-client-trio.ts:314 coerces via `asJsonInput(data.preferenceValue)`, which accepts strings (parsed/passed as JSON), numbers, booleans, objects, and arrays. The adapter file header explicitly documents this mismatch (lines 19-21). Long-term tech debt: when manifest IR grammar gains an `object` kind, align manifest type to `object` and remove this allowlist entry — until then, allowlist is the only option per the audit's documented resolution paths.

viewport.rule: Prisma field is `viewport Json` (no @map) — JSON storage. Manifest declares `viewport: string = "{}"` because the manifest IR grammar has no `object` kind yet. CommandBoardLayoutPrismaStore.create in packages/manifest-adapters/src/prisma-stores/broken-read-batch05-command-board.ts:306 coerces via `asJsonInput(data.viewport)` so JSON objects round-trip cleanly while string defaults like "{}" are stored as valid JSON strings. The adapter file header documents this handling (lines 16-19). Long-term tech debt: same as ClientPreference.preferenceValue — blocked on manifest IR grammar gaining an `object` kind.
